description_long: This is a stately bank building with a neoclassical architectural style, featuring a symmetrical facade and a prominent triangular pediment above the main entrance. The front is adorned with four large columns that support the pediment, giving the structure a grand and authoritative appearance. The facade is punctuated by evenly spaced rectangular windows, arranged in three rows, and three sets of double doors at the entrance, each with arched transom windows above. The building sits on a raised, dark stone platform with wide steps leading up to the entrance. The roof is hipped with a central gable, and the exterior walls are finished in a light stone or concrete material. No visible signage, ATMs, or overt security features are present on the exterior.
description: A grand neoclassical bank building with a columned entrance, triangular pediment, and symmetrical rows of windows on a raised stone platform.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance with three double doors, four large columns, a triangular pediment, and multiple rows of windows.
description_view_1: The right side view displays the solid side wall and the hipped roof, with no windows or doors visible.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a plain, windowless wall and the continuation of the raised stone base.
description_view_3: The left side view mirrors the right, showing a solid wall and the hipped roof without any openings.
attribute_entrance_design: Three sets of double doors made of dark material, each with an arched transom window above, framed by four large columns and accessed via wide stone steps.
